name: Enterprise Architecture Core Ontology
description: Platform-agnostic enterprise architecture ontology covering TOGAF architecture domains (Business, Data, Application, Technology, Security, Integration), core EA entities, governance constructs (principles, decisions, roadmaps), and capability gap analysis. Provides the foundation for cloud-platform-specific extensions (Microsoft, AWS, GCP).

oaa:imports.name: ORG-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Organisation foundation ontology providing OrganizationalUnit for EA entity management responsibility

oaa:imports.name: VSOM-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Vision-Strategy-Objectives-Metrics ontology providing strategic alignment anchors

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Strategic Alignment Join
oaa:joinPatterns.description: EA entities align to VSOM strategic objectives for traceability from architecture to strategy
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Strategic alignment reporting and architecture value justification

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Organisation Management Join
oaa:joinPatterns.description: EA entities are managed by ORG organisational units for accountability

entities.name: EAEntity
entities.description: Base class for all enterprise architecture entities. Represents any element within the enterprise architecture that can be described, governed, and related to other elements across architecture domains.

oaa:properties.name: lifecycleStatus
oaa:properties.description: Current lifecycle phase of the entity

oaa:properties.name: totalCostOfOwnership
oaa:properties.description: Annual TCO for the entity in base currency

entities.name: BusinessArchitecture
entities.description: TOGAF Business Architecture domain covering strategy, governance, organisation, and business capabilities. Contains entities that define what the business does and how it creates value.

entities.name: DataArchitecture
entities.description: Data Architecture domain covering the structure of logical and physical data assets, data management resources, data governance policies, and data lifecycle management.

entities.name: ApplicationArchitecture
entities.description: Application Architecture domain providing a blueprint for individual applications, their interactions, and relationships to core business processes and data entities.

entities.name: TechnologyArchitecture
entities.description: Technology Architecture domain covering the logical software and hardware capabilities required to support business, data, and application services including platforms and infrastructure.

entities.name: SecurityArchitecture
entities.description: Cross-cutting security domain covering identity management, access control, compliance requirements, threat management, and security control implementation across all architecture layers.

entities.name: IntegrationArchitecture
entities.description: Cross-cutting integration domain covering APIs, event streams, data flows, integration patterns, and the connectivity fabric between enterprise systems and services.

entities.name: BusinessCapability
entities.description: A particular ability or capacity that a business possesses or exchanges to achieve a specific purpose. Capabilities are measured by maturity level and criticality, and are realised by applications.

entities.name: BusinessProcess
entities.description: A collection of related, structured activities that produce a specific service or product for a particular customer or market. Processes support business capabilities and are managed by organisational units.

entities.name: Application
entities.description: A software system that supports business functions, automates processes, and provides services. Applications realise business capabilities and are hosted on technology platforms.

oaa:properties.name: technologyFit
oaa:properties.description: Assessment of technology fit to purpose

entities.name: DataEntity
entities.description: A fundamental business data object representing a core concept in the enterprise data model. Data entities are managed under data governance policies and consumed by applications.

entities.name: ArchitecturePrinciple
entities.description: A fundamental guideline intended to be enduring and seldom amended that informs and supports the way in which an organisation sets about fulfilling its mission through architecture decisions.

oaa:properties.name: rationale
oaa:properties.description: Reasoning behind why this principle is important

entities.name: ArchitectureDecision
entities.description: A recorded architectural decision with rationale, alternatives considered, and status. Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) provide traceability for significant choices that shape the enterprise architecture.

oaa:properties.name: status
oaa:properties.description: Decision status: Proposed, Accepted, Superseded, Deprecated

entities.name: ArchitectureRoadmap
entities.description: A timeline of planned architecture transitions from baseline to target state, identifying capability gaps, work packages, and transition architectures required to achieve the desired future state.

entities.name: CapabilityGap
entities.description: An identified gap between current and target capability states, quantified by maturity levels and linked to architecture roadmap items for remediation planning.

entities.name: OrganizationalUnit
entities.description: External ORG-ONT entity representing an organisational unit responsible for managing EA entities and architecture governance.

entities.name: StrategicObjective
entities.description: External VSOM-ONT entity representing a strategic objective that EA entities align to for strategy-architecture traceability.

relationships.name: composedOf
relationships.description: An EA entity is composed of other EA entities, forming a hierarchical decomposition (e.g., architecture domain composed of capabilities, application composed of components)

relationships.name: dependsOn
relationships.description: An EA entity depends on another entity for its operation, creating a dependency chain for impact analysis and change management

relationships.name: realizes
relationships.description: An application realises one or more business capabilities, providing the technology enablement for organisational abilities

relationships.name: supports
relationships.description: An EA entity supports another entity in achieving its purpose, representing enabling relationships between architecture elements

relationships.name: hostedOn
relationships.description: An application is hosted on a technology architecture element, establishing the runtime platform dependency

relationships.name: governs
relationships.description: An architecture principle governs EA entities, establishing governance constraints and guidelines that shape architecture decisions

relationships.name: implements
relationships.description: An EA entity implements a standard, pattern, or another entity's specification, establishing traceability from design to implementation

relationships.name: securedBy
relationships.description: An EA entity is secured by security architecture controls, establishing security coverage across the enterprise

relationships.name: managedBy
relationships.description: An EA entity is managed by an organisational unit, establishing ownership and accountability for architecture elements

relationships.name: alignsTo
relationships.description: An EA entity aligns to a VSOM strategic objective, ensuring architecture investments are traceable to strategic outcomes

relationships.name: integratesWith
relationships.description: An EA entity integrates with another entity via APIs, events, or data flows, establishing the integration fabric between systems

relationships.name: identifiesGap
relationships.description: An architecture roadmap identifies capability gaps that need to be addressed through planned transitions and investments

enumerations.name: LifecycleStatus
enumerations.description: Lifecycle phases for EA entities from initial proposal through retirement

hasDefinedTerm.name: Proposed
hasDefinedTerm.description: Entity proposed, not yet approved for development

hasDefinedTerm.name: Development
hasDefinedTerm.description: Entity under active development or implementation

hasDefinedTerm.name: Deprecated
hasDefinedTerm.description: Entity scheduled for retirement, replacement planned

hasDefinedTerm.name: Retired
hasDefinedTerm.description: Entity decommissioned and no longer operational

enumerations.name: BusinessCriticality
enumerations.description: Criticality ratings for business impact assessment and prioritisation

hasDefinedTerm.name: Critical
hasDefinedTerm.description: Severe business impact, no workarounds possible

enumerations.name: MaturityLevel
enumerations.description: Capability maturity levels based on CMMI scale for gap analysis

enumerations.name: TechnologyFit
enumerations.description: Assessment of how well technology serves its intended purpose

hasDefinedTerm.name: Good
hasDefinedTerm.description: Meets requirements well, minor enhancements possible

businessRules.name: BusinessCapabilityDomainAssignment
businessRules.description: Every business capability must belong to exactly one architecture domain via composedOf to ensure complete capability coverage
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST be referenced by exactly one architecture domain entity via ea-core:composedOf

businessRules.name: ApplicationCapabilityRealisation
businessRules.description: Every application must realise at least one business capability to justify its existence in the portfolio
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one ea-core:realizes relationship to a ea-core:BusinessCapability

businessRules.name: ArchitectureDecisionRationale
businessRules.description: Architecture decisions must document rationale for traceability and governance review
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have a non-empty rationale property

businessRules.name: CapabilityGapProgression
businessRules.description: Capability gaps must show improvement direction with target state higher than current state
businessRules.condition: IF ea-core:CapabilityGap has currentState and targetState properties
businessRules.action: THEN targetState MUST be greater than currentState

businessRules.name: PrincipleGovernanceCoverage
businessRules.description: Architecture principles must govern at least one entity to demonstrate active governance
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one ea-core:governs relationship

businessRules.name: DeprecatedApplicationReplacement
businessRules.description: Deprecated applications should reference their planned replacement for migration planning
businessRules.action: THEN it SHOULD have a ea-core:dependsOn reference to its replacement application

businessRules.name: CriticalEntitySecurityCoverage
businessRules.description: Critical business entities must have security coverage to meet risk management requirements
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one ea-core:securedBy relationship to ea-core:SecurityArchitecture

businessRules.name: RoadmapGapIdentification
businessRules.description: Architecture roadmaps must identify at least one capability gap to drive transition planning
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one ea-core:identifiesGap relationship to ea-core:CapabilityGap

changeControl.changeNotes: Initial creation from EA-ONT V3 source consolidation. Platform-agnostic core extracted from ea-azure-ontology-v2-pre-oaav5, covering 7 architecture domains, 15 entities, 12 relationships, 8 business rules, and 4 enumerations.

versionHistory.notes: Initial release: Platform-agnostic EA core from V3 source consolidation
